mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.5, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts signal synchronized real-economy expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 50 basis points over 20-day windows in 75% of rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound environment, persistent core inflation above 2.0 percent forces market expectations of premature policy normalization, compressing equity multiples and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered episodes, or the signal's information coefficient decays to zero after adjusting for volatility.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction permits reflect forward-looking consumer confidence and credit availability, directly supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon through wealth and multiplier effects.
failure_condition: Weekly forward returns show no statistically significant correlation with HOUST month-over-month changes (p > 0.10), or the signal reverses direction during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in industrial production exceeding 1.5 percent over three months indicates supply-side bottlenecks or demand collapse, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon as earnings revisions turn adverse.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered contraction episodes, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels above 25.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market normalization, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate positive risk-adjusted returns over 60-day windows in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth paired with elevated housing starts signals a self-reinforcing demand expansion, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below chance levels when GDP growth exceeds 4.0 percent annualized.
